MINI MINUTES: Appearance:

Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant General Motors Corporation's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Judicial Council Form Interrogatories and Special Interrogatories, Document Production Requests, And To Deem Request For Admissions is moot as supplemental responses have been served. The court will not determine the adequacy of those responses on this motion. The parties are required to meet and confer and to complete that meet and confer prior to January 4, 2006. Plaintiffs are advised the the supplemental responses are not likely to be found sufficient. Responses which incorporate other responses are not adequate. Defendants are advised the objections based upon privilege are not likely to be found to have been waived. The court finds that the inadequate responses and late supplemental responses were a misuse of the discovery process. Sanctions are imposed against Plaintiffs in the amount of $786.30, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Order signed in open court. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
